title: MSc Study at the College of Artificial Intelligence
content: The Master of Science (M.Sc.) in Artificial Intelligence at AASTMT (New El-Alamein City/Alexandria) is a research-focused program designed to prepare technology leaders. The curriculum covers core and emerging fields including Machine Learning, Computer Vision, Robotics, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Digital Twins, and Explainable AI (XAI). The program operates on a credit-hour system with a flexible duration of 2 to 5 years and follows a hybrid learning model (60-70% face-to-face and 30-40% remote). Graduation requires the completion of 36 credit hours, maintaining a minimum GPA of 2.2, and publishing at least one research paper in a Q3 (or higher) Scopus or Web of Science indexed journal.

title: MSc Admission Checklist
content: Applicants must submit the following documents to the Office of the Vice Dean for Postgraduate Studies: 1. Official academic transcripts from all prior institutions. 2. A formal explanation of the grading scale used by those institutions. 3. Certified 'True Copies' of all submitted documents (non-refundable). 4. A clear copy of Passport or National ID. 5. A fully completed application form. 6. A financial plan for sponsored or funded applicants.

title: MSc Tuition & Fees Framework
content: Tuition fees are calculated based on the number of registered credit hours. Fees for each course must be paid at the start of every semester, either in full or per registered hour. Late payments result in registration cancellation and being barred from lectures. Partial refunds are only available if a course is dropped within the first 3 weeks of the semester. Scholarships and discounts (such as sibling discounts) are available according to Arab Academic Regulations.

title: MSc Program Structure and Academic Requirements
content: The program consists of 36 credit hours: 2 core courses (6 hours), 4 elective courses (12 hours), and a Research Thesis (18 hours). Thesis registration is permitted after successfully passing 12 credit hours of coursework and presenting a research proposal in a formal seminar. The primary language of instruction and thesis writing is English. Admission requires a minimum GPA of 2.40/4.00 (or 'Good' grade). Graduates from non-computing Engineering or Science majors may be required to pass a qualifying exam or take supplementary bridge courses.

title: Graduation Requirements and Academic Integrity
content: To graduate, students must publish at least one research paper in a Q3 or higher journal indexed in Web of Science or Scopus before their final defense. Course evaluation is based on 60% coursework and 40% final exam, with a minimum passing grade of 60% per course. The college enforces a zero-tolerance policy regarding plagiarism and academic dishonesty; failure to uphold these standards results in immediate dismissal from the program.

title: Program Description
content: The Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence is offered under AASTMT's College of Artificial Intelligence, one of the first specialized AI colleges in the Arab region, established in 2019 at the El Alamein campus. The program is designed to equip graduate students with both foundational and advanced knowledge in AI, preparing them for leadership roles in research, industry, and academia.

title: Admission Requirements
content: Applicants must hold a bachelor's degree with a minimum GPA of 2.40/4.00 or a 'Good' overall grade. Applicants with a 'Pass' grade may be accepted provided they already possess an academic diploma in the same specialization with a grade of at least 'Good.' The program is open to graduates of faculties of Computer Science and Information Technology, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Engineering, and the Computer Science division within the Faculty of Science. Graduates of other Engineering or Science specializations may be admitted after passing a qualifying exam or completing supplementary courses determined by the faculty. Holders of a bachelor's degree in Management Information Systems are also eligible, provided they possess an equivalent academic diploma with a grade of 'Good.'

title: Official Documents Required for Admission
content: Applicants must submit the following documents to the Office of the Vice Dean for Graduate Studies and Research at the El Alamein Branch: Official Academic Transcripts from each college or university previously attended; a Grading Scale Explanation including a clear explanation of the evaluation scale used by previous institutions; officially certified copies of all documents (non-returnable); a clear copy of a valid passport or national ID card; a fully completed admission application form; and a financial plan if the applicant is sponsored or funded by a specific entity or scholarship program.

title: Guidelines for Tuition & Fees
content: The following regulatory rules—approved by the Dean of Admissions and Registration—govern all tuition and fee-related matters: Tuition fees are determined based on the number of registered credit hours. Fees for each course must be paid at the beginning of each semester. Payment can be made in full for the entire semester or according to the number of registered credit hours. Failure to pay tuition fees on time will result in the student's registration being cancelled and being barred from attending lectures. A partial refund is applicable only if a course is dropped within the first 3 weeks of the semester.

title: Academic Structure & Specializations
content: The Master’s program operates on a 36-credit-hour system: 2 core courses (6 hours), 4 elective courses (12 hours), and a two-part research thesis (18 hours). Specializations include Natural Language Processing (NLP), Computer Vision, Robotics, Embedded AI, Cognitive Systems, and emerging fields such as Digital Twins, AI in Pharmacy/Biotechnology, and Explainable AI (XAI). Instruction and the research thesis are primarily in English, with Arabic allowed only in exceptional cases with board approval.

title: MSc Admission & Administrative Requirements
content: Admission requires a Bachelor’s degree with a minimum GPA of 2.40/4.00 or a 'Good' grade. Eligible graduates include those from Computer Science, AI, Computer Engineering, or Science (CS major). Other Engineering/Science graduates must pass a qualifying exam or complete remedial courses. Application deadlines are early September (Fall) and early February (Spring). The degree duration is 2 to 5 years, utilizing a hybrid learning format (60–70% face-to-face in New Alamein or Alexandria, and 30–40% distance learning).

title: Graduation Requirements & Academic Integrity
content: To graduate, students must publish at least one research paper in a Scopus or Web of Science indexed journal (minimum Q3 ranking) before the final seminar. Thesis registration requires passing 12 credit hours and a formal proposal seminar. Course evaluation is split: 60% coursework and 40% final exam, with a 60% minimum passing grade. The college maintains a zero-tolerance policy for plagiarism; any violation results in immediate dismissal.

title: General Information, Fees & Contact
content: Tuition fees are calculated per registered credit hour and set each semester. Partial refunds are only available within the first 3 weeks of withdrawal. Scholarships and discounts (e.g., sibling discounts) are available per Arab Academic regulations. Graduates receive a Master of Science (M.Sc.) in Artificial Intelligence. For inquiries, contact the Office of the Vice Dean for Postgraduate Studies at the Alamein branch via WhatsApp: +201093231831.

title: Specific Academic Milestones
content: Graduation requires a minimum cumulative GPA of 2.2. The Research Thesis registration is officially permitted only after the successful completion of 12 credit hours of coursework. If a thesis is granted exceptional approval to be written in Arabic, it must include a comprehensive bilingual summary in both Arabic and English.

title: Faculty and Supervision
content: The program is delivered by a specialized group of Professors and Assistant Professors from Egyptian universities. For each researcher, the College Board appoints a main supervisor based on the Department Committee's recommendation. The supervision committee may be modified later based on the student's research progress and the Board's approval.

title: Detailed Eligibility for 'Pass' Degree Holders
content: Applicants with a 'Pass' (Satisfactory) general grade are only eligible for admission if they have previously obtained an 'Academic Diploma' in the same specialization. This diploma must have been completed with a minimum grade of 'Good'.

title: Digital Application & Research Timeline
content: An online application portal is currently under development and will be available soon. Regarding research, the mandatory publication in a Scopus/Web of Science (Min. Q3) journal must be completed specifically before the student's final seminar/defense session.
